verdict.answer_first: B: sentence 1 says the bottom line, "Yes — the main problem is using floating-point math for money"; A also leads well with "The main problem is...", but B is sharper.
verdict.decisiveness: A is slightly more disciplined: it names one main problem and keeps other issues secondary; B weakens the prescription by adding "Or, better... or use a decimal library".
verdict.fabrication: none; both infer money from "priceFloat" and use standard IEEE-754 examples, not invented project facts.
verdict.actionability: B: gives a direct replacement returning integer cents, "return items.reduce((s, i) => s + Math.round(i.priceFloat * 100), 0)".
